description: This stacked horizontal bar chart displays the oxide proportion of passivation films for Fe–30Mn–8Al–C-xCu steels, specifically the 0Cu and 3Cu samples labeled on the y-axis. As reported in the accompanying text, these films were formed under potentiostatic polarization at −0.3 V (vs. Ag–AgCl) in a 3.5 wt% NaCl solution for 7200 s. The lower bar (0Cu) shows 4.8% Fe₃O₄, 7.4% Fe₂O₃, 5.9% FeOOH, 35.5% MnO, 30% MnO₂, 10.9% Al₂O₃, and 5.5% Al. The upper bar (3Cu) shows 4.3% Fe₃O₄, 6.7% Fe₂O₃, 5.4% FeOOH, 43.5% MnO, 24.6% MnO₂, 8.1% Al₂O₃, 5.3% Al, 1.1% Cu₂O, and 1% CuO. The chart indicates that 0Cu retains higher Fe and Al oxide contents, consistent with a more stable protective film, whereas 3Cu exhibits a higher Mn oxide fraction and contains Cu oxides. The related text interprets this contrast as evidence that Cu alloying promotes formation of an unstable oxide film and intensifies galvanic corrosion, thereby accelerating anodic dissolution and degrading passivation stability.
